18	)

20	// Load loads the yaml and fulfills unspecified fields with the default values.
21	//
22	// Load does not validate. Use Validate for validation.
23	func Load(ctx context.Context, b []byte, filePath string) (*limatype.LimaYAML, error) {
24		return load(ctx, b, filePath, false)
25	}
26	
27	// LoadWithWarnings will call FillDefaults with warnings enabled (e.g. when
28	// the username is not valid on Linux and must be replaced by "Lima").
29	// It is called when creating or editing an instance.
30	func LoadWithWarnings(ctx context.Context, b []byte, filePath string) (*limatype.LimaYAML, error) {
31		return load(ctx, b, filePath, true)
32	}
33	
34	func load(ctx context.Context, b []byte, filePath string, warn bool) (*limatype.LimaYAML, error) {
35		var y, d, o limatype.LimaYAML
36	
37		if err := Unmarshal(b, &y, fmt.Sprintf("main file %q", filePath)); err != nil {
38			return nil, err
39		}
40		configDir, err := dirnames.LimaConfigDir()
41		if err != nil {
42			return nil, err
43		}
44	
45		defaultPath := filepath.Join(configDir, filenames.Default)
46		bytes, err := os.ReadFile(defaultPath)
47		if err == nil {
48			logrus.Debugf("Mixing %q into %q", defaultPath, filePath)
49			if err := Unmarshal(bytes, &d, fmt.Sprintf("default file %q", defaultPath)); err != nil {
50				return nil, err
51			}
52		} else if !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
53			return nil, err
54		}
55	
56		overridePath := filepath.Join(configDir, filenames.Override)
57		bytes, err = os.ReadFile(overridePath)
58		if err == nil {
59			logrus.Debugf("Mixing %q into %q", overridePath, filePath)
60			if err := Unmarshal(bytes, &o, fmt.Sprintf("override file %q", overridePath)); err != nil {
61				return nil, err
62			}
63		} else if !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
64			return nil, err
65		}
66	
67		// It should be called before the `y` parameter is passed to FillDefault() that execute template.
68		if err := validateParamIsUsed(&y); err != nil {
69			return nil, err
70		}
71	
72		FillDefault(ctx, &y, &d, &o, filePath, warn)
73	
74		return &y, nil
75	}
76

2	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
3	
4	package limayaml
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"errors"

12		"path"

17		"strconv"

21		"github.com/docker/go-units"
22		"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
23	
24		"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/driverutil"
25		"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/identifiers"
26		"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/limatype"
27		"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/localpathutil"
28		"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/networks"
29		"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/osutil"
30		"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/version"
31		"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/version/versionutil"
32	)
33	
34	func Validate(y *limatype.LimaYAML, warn bool) error {
35		var errs error
36	
37		if len(y.Base) > 0 {
38			errs = errors.Join(errs, errors.New("field `base` must be empty for YAML validation"))
39		}
40	
41		if y.MinimumLimaVersion != nil {
42			if _, err := versionutil.Parse(*y.MinimumLimaVersion); err != nil {
43				errs = errors.Join(errs, fmt.Errorf("field `minimumLimaVersion` must be a semvar value, got %q: %w", *y.MinimumLimaVersion, err))
44			}
45			// Unparsable version.Version (like commit hashes or "<unknown>") is treated as "latest/greatest"
46			// and will pass all version comparisons, allowing development builds to work.
47			if !versionutil.GreaterEqual(version.Version, *y.MinimumLimaVersion) {
48				errs = errors.Join(errs, fmt.Errorf("template requires Lima version %q; this is only %q", *y.MinimumLimaVersion, version.Version))
49			}
50		}
51	
52		switch *y.OS {
53		case limatype.LINUX, limatype.DARWIN, limatype.FREEBSD:
54		default:
55			errs = errors.Join(errs, fmt.Errorf("field `os` must be one of %q; got %q", limatype.OSTypes, *y.OS))
56		}
57		if !slices.Contains(limatype.ArchTypes, *y.Arch) {
58			errs = errors.Join(errs, fmt.Errorf("field `arch` must be one of %v; got %q", limatype.ArchTypes, *y.Arch))
59		}
60

18		MinimumLimaVersion *string       `yaml:"minimumLimaVersion,omitempty" json:"minimumLimaVersion,omitempty" jsonschema:"nullable"`
19		VMType             *VMType       `yaml:"vmType,omitempty" json:"vmType,omitempty" jsonschema:"nullable"`

26		CPUs                  *int          `yaml:"cpus,omitempty" json:"cpus,omitempty" jsonschema:"nullable"`
27		Memory                *string       `yaml:"memory,omitempty" json:"memory,omitempty" jsonschema:"nullable"` // go-units.RAMInBytes

30		Mounts                []Mount       `yaml:"mounts,omitempty" json:"mounts,omitempty"`
31		MountTypesUnsupported []string      `yaml:"mountTypesUnsupported,omitempty" json:"mountTypesUnsupported,omitempty" jsonschema:"nullable"`

48		Env          map[string]string `yaml:"env,omitempty" json:"env,omitempty"`

59		NestedVirtualization *bool   `yaml:"nestedVirtualization,omitempty" json:"nestedVirtualization,omitempty" jsonschema:"nullable"`

345	func NewOS(osname string) OS {
346		switch osname {
347		case "linux":
348			return LINUX
349		case "darwin":
350			return DARWIN
351		default:
352			logrus.Warnf("Unknown os: %s", osname)
353			return osname
354		}
355	}
356	
357	func Goarm() int {
358		if runtime.GOOS != "linux" {
359			return 0
360		}
361		if runtime.GOARCH != "arm" {
362			return 0
363		}
364		if cpu.ARM.HasVFPv3 {
365			return 7
366		}
367		if cpu.ARM.HasVFP {
368			return 6
369		}
370		return 5 // default
371	}
372	
373	func NewArch(arch string) Arch {
374		switch arch {
375		case "amd64":
376			return X8664
377		case "arm64":
378			return AARCH64
379		case "arm":
380			arm := Goarm()
381			if arm == 7 {
382				return ARMV7L
383			}
384			logrus.Warnf("Unknown arm: %d", arm)
385			return arch
386		case "ppc64le":
387			return PPC64LE
388		case "riscv64":
389			return RISCV64
390		case "s390x":
391			return S390X
392		default:
393			logrus.Warnf("Unknown arch: %s", arch)
394			return arch
395		}
396	}
397	
398	func IsNativeArch(arch Arch) bool {
399		nativeX8664 := arch == X8664 && runtime.GOARCH == "amd64"
400		nativeAARCH64 := arch == AARCH64 && runtime.GOARCH == "arm64"
401		nativeARMV7L := arch == ARMV7L && runtime.GOARCH == "arm" && Goarm() == 7
402		nativePPC64LE := arch == PPC64LE && runtime.GOARCH == "ppc64le"
403		nativeRISCV64 := arch == RISCV64 && runtime.GOARCH == "riscv64"
404		nativeS390X := arch == S390X && runtime.GOARCH == "s390x"
405		return nativeX8664 || nativeAARCH64 || nativeARMV7L || nativePPC64LE || nativeRISCV64 || nativeS390X
406	}
407	
408	func DefaultDriver() VMType {
409		switch runtime.GOOS {
410		case "darwin":
411			return VZ
412		case "windows":
413			return WSL2
414		default:
415			return QEMU
416		}
417	}
418	
419	func DefaultNonNativeArchDriver() VMType {
420		return QEMU
421	}

file.content:
// SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright The Lima Authors
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

package limayaml

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"runtime"
	"testing"

	"gotest.tools/v3/assert"

	"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/limatype"
)

func dumpJSON(t *testing.T, d any) string {
	b, err := json.Marshal(d)
	assert.NilError(t, err)
	return string(b)
}

const emptyYAML = "{}\n"

func TestEmptyYAML(t *testing.T) {
	var y limatype.LimaYAML
	t.Log(dumpJSON(t, y))
	b, err := Marshal(&y, false)
	assert.NilError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, string(b), emptyYAML)
}

const defaultYAML = "{}\n"

func TestDefaultYAML(t *testing.T) {
	content, err := os.ReadFile("default.yaml")
	assert.NilError(t, err)
	// if this is the unresolved symlink as a file, then make sure to resolve it
	if runtime.GOOS == "windows" && bytes.HasPrefix(content, []byte{'.', '.'}) {
		f, err := filepath.Rel(".", string(content))
		assert.NilError(t, err)
		content, err = os.ReadFile(f)
		assert.NilError(t, err)
	}

	var y limatype.LimaYAML
	err = Unmarshal(content, &y, "")
	assert.NilError(t, err)
	y.Images = nil                // remove default images
	y.Mounts = nil                // remove default mounts
	y.Base = nil                  // remove default base templates
	y.VMOpts = nil                // remove default vmopts mapping
	y.MinimumLimaVersion = nil    // remove minimum Lima version
	y.MountTypesUnsupported = nil // remove default workaround for kernel 6.9-6.11
	t.Log(dumpJSON(t, y))
	b, err := Marshal(&y, false)
	assert.NilError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, string(b), defaultYAML)
}

content.text:
Now I can see the issue clearly. Looking at the `mergeDocuments` yq script in `embed.go`, on line 228:

```
| $a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)
```

This line says: if the template (`$a`) has a `.dns` field, delete the DNS from the base (`$b`) and from `$c`. The logic here is reversed! 

The DNS logic should be: if the template already has DNS entries, keep them and ignore the base's DNS. But the current behavior is:
- Line 228: If `$a` has `.dns`, delete DNS from `$b` and `$c` → correct, this would prevent the base DNS from being copied

But wait, let me re-read the `FillDefault` function more carefully. The `FillDefault` function handles merging `y` (main), `d` (default), and `o` (override). But the base templates are already resolved via `embed.go` BEFORE `FillDefault` is called. Let me check the flow.
Now let me look at where `Embed` is called and how the base templates are processed end-to-end. Let me trace from start.go:

Now I understand the flow. The `Embed` function resolves base templates by using yq expressions. Let me look at the `mergeDocuments` script more carefully. 

The key issue is on line 228 of `embed.go`:

```
| $a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)
```

This says: if the **template** has DNS entries, delete them from the base. This is the **opposite** of what's needed. The intended behavior (based on the comment on line 207: "dns lists are not merged and only copied when the template doesn't have any dns entries at all") is: if the template already has DNS entries, keep them; if the template does NOT have DNS entries, copy them from the base. But the current line deletes base DNS when the template has DNS.

Wait, let me re-think. After line 228, all fields that don't exist in `$a` are copied from `$c` (which is `$b` minus what's in `$a`). So if we delete `$b.dns` (and `$c.dns`), that means DNS won't be copied from base. But the line says: "if `$a` has `.dns`, delete `$b.dns`". That means: "if template has DNS, don't copy base DNS". That sounds correct semantically.

But wait - the issue says DNS settings are NOT propagated from base template to main template. So the DNS in the base template is NOT showing up in the main template. This means the DNS from the base IS being deleted (when it shouldn't be) or NOT being copied (when it should be).

Let me re-read the logic more carefully:

1. `$c` is a copy of `$b` (the base)
2. Line 228: `| $a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)` — If `$a` HAS `.dns`, delete DNS from both `$b` and `$c`
3. Line 232: `| $c | select(tag == "!!seq") tag = "!!tag"` — mark all list fields in `$c` with "!!tag"
4. Line 236: `| $c | delpaths([$a | .. | select(tag != "!!map") | path])` — delete all nodes in `$c` that exist in `$a`
5. Line 239: `| $a * ($c // {}) as $a` — merge from `$c` into `$a` (only new fields from `$c` are merged)

So the flow for DNS is:
- If `$a` has `.dns`, line 228 deletes `.dns` from both `$b` and `$c`. This means base DNS won't be copied. ✓ Correct.
- If `$a` does NOT have `.dns`, line 228 does nothing. DNS remains in `$b` and `$c`. Line 236 deletes from `$c` nodes that exist in `$a`. Since `$a` doesn't have DNS, `.dns` stays in `$c`. Line 239 merges it into `$a`. ✓ Correct.

content.text:
Now I understand the full flow:

1. In `start.go`, `limatmpl.Read` reads the template, then `tmpl.Embed` resolves base templates (merging them via yq). After `Embed`, the template bytes (`tmpl.Bytes`) contain the merged YAML with base templates resolved.
2. Then these bytes (`tmpl.Bytes`) are passed to `instance.Create`, which calls `limayaml.LoadWithWarnings(ctx, instConfig, filePath)`.
3. `LoadWithWarnings` calls `load` which calls `FillDefault`.

So the DNS from the base should already be in `tmpl.Bytes` after `Embed` resolves base templates. The yq `mergeDocuments` script should copy the DNS from base.

Let me look more carefully at the yq script to understand what's happening. Let me focus on the `mergeDocuments` more carefully:

Line 228: `| $a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)`

This says: If `$a` (current template) has `.dns`, delete `.dns` from both `$b` (base) and `$c` (copy of base for merge). The intent is "if the template already specifies DNS, ignore base DNS".

But there's a subtlety. After this line, the fields from `$c` that don't exist in `$a` get merged into `$a`. But `.dns` IS a seq (list). Let me trace the flow more carefully:

Line 232: `| $c | .. | select(tag == "!!seq") tag = "!!tag"` - marks ALL seq in $c with "!!tag"

Line 236: `| $c | delpaths([$a | .. | select(tag != "!!map") | path])` - delete from $c nodes that exist in $a (not maps)

Line 239: `| $a * ($c // {}) as $a` - merge $c into $a (only new fields)

Line 245-248: Append lists from $b to existing lists in $a (except base, provision, probes which are prepended).

So if $a doesn't have `.dns`, after line 228 the `.dns` remains in $b and $c. Then line 236 would NOT delete `.dns` from $c (because $a doesn't have `.dns`). Then line 239 would copy `.dns` from $c to $a. Then line 245 would try to append `.dns` from $b to $a.

Wait - but I need to check if `|| test(...) | not` logic on line 245 applies to `.dns`. The expression `test("^(base|provision|probes)$")` only checks for `base`, `provision`, and `probes` at the root path. `.dns` wouldn't match this, so DNS lists from $b ARE appended to $a.

But what if $a already has `.dns` as an empty list? In the YAML, `dns: []` would create `.dns` as an empty list. Then `select(.dns)` on line 228 would match (because `.dns` exists even if empty), and `$b.dns` would be deleted. That's correct behavior - if the user explicitly sets `dns: []` in the main template, they don't want DNS from the base.

But what if the main template only has `base: [base/debian.yaml]` and no `dns:` field at all? Then `select(.dns)` should be false and DNS from the base should be copied.

Wait, I need to check this more carefully. Let me look at the `mergeDocuments` script from line 211 again. Let me also check if there's a `defaultBase` handling issue. The `Embed` function is called with `embedAll=true, defaultBase=true`. Look at lines 32-45 of embed.go:

```go
func (tmpl *Template) Embed(ctx context.Context, embedAll, defaultBase bool) error {
    ...
    err := tmpl.embedAllBases(ctx, embedAll, defaultBase, seen)
    ...
}
```

And in `embedAllBases` (lines 47-108):
```go
if defaultBase {
    configDir, err := dirnames.LimaConfigDir()
    ...
    defaultBaseFilename := filepath.Join(configDir, filenames.Base)
    if _, err := os.Stat(defaultBaseFilename); err == nil {
        // prepend base template at the beginning of the list
        fmt.Fprintf(&tmpl.expr, "| $a.base = [%q, $a.base[]]\n", defaultBaseFilename)
        if err := tmpl.evalExpr(); err != nil {
            return err
        }
    }
}
```

So the `defaultBase` adds `_config/base.yaml` as a base template if it exists. But that's a separate file system default base - not the same as `base:` in the YAML.

Now let me think again about the issue. The problem from the bug report is that when a main template has only:
```yaml
base:
  - base/debian.yaml
```
And the base template has `dns:` set, the DNS doesn't propagate.

Hmm, actually wait. The main template has `base:` as a seq. After the `mergeDocuments` script runs:

Line 219: `| $a | select(.base | tag == "!!seq") | del(.base[0])` - deletes `base[0]` from $a
Line 220: `| $a | select(.base | (tag == "!!seq" and length == 0)) | del(.base)` - deletes entire `.base` if empty
Line 221: `| $a | select(.base | tag == "!!str") | del(.base)` - deletes `.base` if it's a string

After removing `base[0]`, the `.base` list should be empty (since the main template only has one base entry). So `.base` gets deleted entirely. That's fine.

But what about the DNS? After the merge, the base template's `.dns` field should be in $b. Since $a only has `base:` originally, it doesn't have `.dns`. So `select(.dns)` on line 228 should NOT trigger. Then DNS from $b should flow through to $a via the generic merge logic.

Hmm, let me look at this from another angle. Maybe the issue is NOT in the `mergeDocuments` yq script, but in the `FillDefault` function. Let me look at lines 781-787 again:

```go
// Note: DNS lists are not combined; highest priority setting is picked
if len(y.DNS) == 0 {
    y.DNS = d.DNS
}
if len(o.DNS) > 0 {
    y.DNS = o.DNS
}
```

Here, `d` is from `_config/default.yaml` (a separate `default.yaml` file, not the base template). If the user has a default.yaml file with DNS set, then even if the base template provided DNS through the yq merge, the `d.DNS` would be empty (assuming default.yaml doesn't set DNS). So `y.DNS` from the base template would stay. But what if there IS a default.yaml with DNS entries? Then `d.DNS` would override the base template DNS because `FillDefault` only checks `y.DNS == 0`, not whether the current y.DNS came from a base template or not.

Wait, actually that shouldn't matter because `d` (default.yaml) is different from the `base` templates. The base templates are resolved through yq before `FillDefault` is called.

729	func TestStaticPortForwarding(t *testing.T) {
730		tests := []struct {

732			config   limatype.LimaYAML
733			expected []limatype.PortForward
734		}{
735			{
736				name: "plain mode with static port forwards",

739					PortForwards: []limatype.PortForward{
740						{

743							Static:    true,
744						},
745						{
746							GuestPort: 9000,
747							HostPort:  9000,
748							Static:    false,
749						},
750						{
751							GuestPort: 8081,
752							HostPort:  8081,
753						},
754					},
755				},
756				expected: []limatype.PortForward{
757					{
758						GuestPort: 8080,
759						HostPort:  8080,
760						Static:    true,
761					},
762				},
763			},
764			{
765				name: "non-plain mode with static port forwards",
766				config: limatype.LimaYAML{
767					Plain: ptr.Of(false),
768					PortForwards: []limatype.PortForward{
769						{
770							GuestPort: 8080,
771							HostPort:  8080,
772							Static:    true,
773						},
774						{
775							GuestPort: 9000,
776							HostPort:  9000,
777							Static:    false,
778						},
779					},
780				},
781				expected: []limatype.PortForward{
782					{
783						GuestPort: 8080,
784						HostPort:  8080,
785						Static:    true,
786					},
787					{
788						GuestPort: 9000,
789						HostPort:  9000,
790						Static:    false,
791					},
792				},
793			},
794			{
795				name: "plain mode with no static port forwards",
796				config: limatype.LimaYAML{
797					Plain: ptr.Of(true),
798					PortForwards: []limatype.PortForward{
799						{
800							GuestPort: 8080,
801							HostPort:  8080,
802							Static:    false,
803						},
804						{
805							GuestPort: 9000,
806							HostPort:  9000,
807						},
808					},
809				},
810				expected: []limatype.PortForward{},
811			},
812		}
813	
814		for _, tt := range tests {
815			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
816				fixUpForPlainMode(&tt.config)
817	
818				if *tt.config.Plain {
819					for _, pf := range tt.config.PortForwards {
820						if !pf.Static {
821							t.Errorf("Non-static port forward found in plain mode: %+v", pf)
822						}
823					}
824				}
825	
826				assert.Equal(t, len(tt.config.PortForwards), len(tt.expected),
827					"Expected %d port forwards, got %d", len(tt.expected), len(tt.config.PortForwards))
828	
829				for i, expected := range tt.expected {
830					if i >= len(tt.config.PortForwards) {
831						t.Errorf("Missing port forward at index %d", i)
832						continue
833					}
834					actual := tt.config.PortForwards[i]
835					assert.Equal(t, expected.Static, actual.Static,
836						"Port forward %d: expected Static=%v, got %v", i, expected.Static, actual.Static)
837					assert.Equal(t, expected.GuestPort, actual.GuestPort,
838						"Port forward %d: expected GuestPort=%d, got %d", i, expected.GuestPort, actual.GuestPort)
839					assert.Equal(t, expected.HostPort, actual.HostPort,
840						"Port forward %d: expected HostPort=%d, got %d", i, expected.HostPort, actual.HostPort)
841				}
842			})
843		}
844	}

856		}

861				assert.Assert(t, len(tag) > 5 && tag[:5] == "lima-")
862				assert.Assert(t, len(tag) <= 36)

864			})

869		mounts := []struct{ location, mountPoint string }{
870			{"/Users/user1", "/Users/user1"},
871			{"/Users/user2", "/Users/user2"},
872			{"/home/user1", "/home/user1"},
873			{"/mnt/data", "/mnt/data"},
874			{"/tmp/lima", "/tmp/lima"},
875		}
876		tags := make(map[string]bool)
877		for _, m := range mounts {
878			tag := MountTag(m.location, m.mountPoint)
879			assert.Assert(t, !tags[tag], "tag collision: %s", tag)
880			tags[tag] = true
881		}
882	}

content.text:
Now I understand the entire flow. The key insight is:

1. `Embed` (in `embed.go`) uses `mergeDocuments` yq script to merge base templates INTO the main template YAML bytes
2. After `Embed`, the `tmpl.Bytes` contain the merged YAML (with base DNS included)
3. Then `Load` is called on these bytes in `create.go`, which calls `FillDefault`

The issue could be in the `mergeDocuments` yq script or in `FillDefault`. Let me now look at `mergeDocuments` more carefully, particularly how it handles `.dns`:

Line 228: `| $a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)`

The intent: If $a (template) already has .dns, then delete .dns from $b and $c (to not copy base DNS).

But what if $a does NOT have .dns? Then the `select(.dns)` is false and nothing happens. .dns remains in $b and $c. Then the later merge steps should pick it up.

Wait, but I now realize there's another issue. After `Embed`, the main template's bytes have been updated. But when `FillDefault` is called, the `d` (defaults) are read from `_config/default.yaml`. What if the `default.yaml` file does NOT have DNS, but the `_config/base.yaml` file has DNS?

Looking at `embedAllBases`:
- If `defaultBase` is true, the `_config/base.yaml` file is added as the first base entry
- The `defaultBase` is resolved by the yq merge just like any other base

But `_config/base.yaml` is different from `_config/default.yaml`. `default.yaml` is handled separately in `load.go` as `d`. `base.yaml` is handled via the yq merge in `embedAllBases`.

So the flow is:
1. `limatmpl.Read` reads the main template
2. `tmpl.Embed` resolves base templates (including `_config/base.yaml`) into `tmpl.Bytes`
3. `limayaml.Load` calls `load` which reads `_config/default.yaml` into `d` and calls `FillDefault(ctx, &y, &d, &o, ...)`
4. `FillDefault` applies `d` (default.yaml) values to `y`

Now the question: does `FillDefault` unintentionally clear DNS that was set by the base template merge?

In `FillDefault`, lines 781-787:
```go
if len(y.DNS) == 0 {
    y.DNS = d.DNS
}
if len(o.DNS) > 0 {
    y.DNS = o.DNS
}
```

This only modifies `y.DNS` if it's empty (then copy from `d`), or if `o` has DNS (then copy from `o`). If `y.DNS` already has values from the base template merge, it won't be touched. So `FillDefault` should be fine.

So the issue must be in the `mergeDocuments` yq script! Let me look at the script one more time very carefully.

Actually wait... Let me re-read line 228:
```
| $a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)
```

Actually, I wonder if `select(.dns)` returns true even when `.dns` is empty `[]` or null. Let me think about this...

In the main template, the YAML is:
```yaml
base:
  - base/debian.yaml
```

After `Unmarshal`, does `.dns` exist? If `dns` is not in the YAML at all, then `.dns` field would be nil/empty (Go zero value for `[]net.IP` is nil). `select(.dns)` in yq should return false for nil/null fields.

BUT wait - line 228 uses `select(.dns)`. In yq, `select(.dns)` evaluates the expression `.dns` in a boolean context. For an array/list, if it exists but is empty, it might still be truthy (because the array exists). Actually, in yq/jq, `select(.foo)` checks if the value is not null and not false. An empty array `[]` is truthy.

So if the main template has `dns: []` (explicit empty DNS), then `select(.dns)` would be true, and `$b.dns` and `$c.dns` would be deleted. But the main template in our case only has `base:`, so `.dns` shouldn't exist.

Hmm, let me think about this differently. Let me look at the actual merge flow more carefully using the yq expressions.

Actually, let me look at the `FillDefault` comment again:

```go
// Note: DNS lists are not combined; highest priority setting is picked
```

I see something interesting now. When the base template has `dns:` entries and the main template does NOT have `dns:`, the DNS should be picked up from the base template through the yq merge. But then `FillDefault` might do something unexpected.

Wait, I think I need to look at this from a different angle. Let me check if the issue might be in `FillDefault` when `y.DNS` is from a base template and `d` (default.yaml) has no DNS:

```go

    y.DNS = d.DNS
}
```

If `y.DNS` has values (from base template), it won't be overwritten. Good.

But wait - what if `FillDefault` is not even receiving the DNS from the base template because the yq merge didn't include it? Let me think about `mergeDocuments` again.

Line 232: `| $c | .. | select(tag == "!!seq") tag = "!!tag"` 

This marks ALL lists in $c with "!!tag". Then:

Line 236: `| $c | delpaths([$a | .. | select(tag != "!!map") | path])`

This deletes from $c nodes that exist in $a. Since $a doesn't have `.dns`, `.dns` stays in $c.

Line 239: `| $a * ($c // {}) as $a`

This merges $c into $a. Since `.dns` is in $c but not in $a, `.dns` gets copied to $a. This should work.

But then:

Line 245-248: 
```
| $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (path[0] | test("^(base|provision|probes)$") | not))) |=
   (. + (path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])))
```

This appends lists from $b to existing lists in $a. After the merge on line 239, $a now has `.dns` (from $c). And $b still has `.dns`. So this line would append $b's `.dns` to $a's `.dns`, effectively duplicating the DNS entries!

Wait no - because $a.dns already contains the values from $b.dns (they were copied from $c which is a copy of $b). So the append would double them up. But that's a different bug than what's reported.

Actually, let me re-examine. After line 228, if $a didn't have `.dns`, then $b.dns and $c.dns still exist. But $c was created as a copy of $b (line 216: `| $b as $c`). Then after the full merge pipeline, $a.dns would have the values from $c.dns (which equals $b.dns). Then line 245 would append $b.dns values to $a.dns, effectively doubling.

Hmm, but this assumes `.dns` is tagged as "!!seq" on line 232 and then the tag gets cleared on line 261. Let me re-read:

Line 232: `| $c | .. | select(tag == "!!seq") tag = "!!tag"` - All lists in $c are tagged
Line 261: `| $a | .. | select(tag == "!!tag") tag = ""` - All tagged lists in $a get untagged

After line 239 merges $c into $a, $a now has `.dns` with "!!tag". Then line 245 finds "!!seq" lists (NOT "!!tag"), and appends from $b. But `.dns` in $a is "!!tag" so it wouldn't match line 245. Good.

Then line 261 clears the "!!tag" to "". So $a.dns ends up with the values from $c.dns only (which equals $b.dns). That should be correct.

So the theoretical flow seems correct. Let me actually write a test to see what's happening.

420	func TestEmbed(t *testing.T) {
421		focus := os.Getenv("TEST_FOCUS")
422		for _, tc := range embedTestCases {
423			if focus != "" {
424				if !strings.Contains(tc.description, focus) {
425					continue
426				}
427				logrus.SetLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)
428			}
429			t.Run(tc.description, func(t *testing.T) { RunEmbedTest(t, tc) })
430		}
431		logrus.SetLevel(logrus.InfoLevel)
432	}
433	
434	func RunEmbedTest(t *testing.T, tc embedTestCase) {
435		todo := strings.HasPrefix(tc.description, "TODO")
436		expectError := strings.HasPrefix(tc.description, "ERROR")
437		stringCompare := strings.HasPrefix(tc.template, "#")
438	
439		// Normalize testcase data
440		tc.template = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(tc.template, "#"))
441		tc.base = strings.TrimSpace(tc.base)
442		tc.expected = strings.TrimSpace(tc.expected)
443	
444		// Change to temp directory so all template and script names don't include a slash.
445		t.Chdir(t.TempDir())
446	
447		for i, base := range strings.Split(tc.base, "---\n") {
448			extension := ".yaml"
449			if strings.HasPrefix(base, "#!") {
450				extension = ".sh"
451			}
452			baseFilename := fmt.Sprintf("base%d%s", i, extension)
453			err := os.WriteFile(baseFilename, []byte(base), 0o600)
454			assert.NilError(t, err, tc.description)
455		}
456		tmpl := &Template{
457			Bytes:   fmt.Appendf(nil, "base: base0.yaml\n%s", tc.template),
458			Locator: "tmpl.yaml",
459		}
460		// Don't include `base` property if base0 is a script
461		if strings.HasPrefix(tc.base, "#!") {
462			tmpl.Bytes = []byte(tc.template)
463		}
464		err := tmpl.Embed(t.Context(), false, false)
465		if expectError {
466			assert.ErrorContains(t, err, tc.expected, tc.description)
467			return
468		}
469		assert.NilError(t, err, tc.description)
470	
471		if stringCompare {
472			actual := strings.TrimSpace(string(tmpl.Bytes))
473			if todo {
474				assert.Assert(t, actual != tc.expected, tc.description)
475			} else {
476				assert.Equal(t, actual, tc.expected, tc.description)
477			}
478			return
479		}
480	
481		err = tmpl.Unmarshal()
482		assert.NilError(t, err, tc.description)
483	
484		var expected limatype.LimaYAML
485		err = limayaml.Unmarshal([]byte(tc.expected), &expected, "expected")
486		assert.NilError(t, err, tc.description)
487	
488		if todo {
489			// using reflect.DeepEqual because cmp.DeepEqual can't easily be negated
490			assert.Assert(t, !reflect.DeepEqual(tmpl.Config, &expected), tc.description)
491		} else {
492			assert.Assert(t, cmp.DeepEqual(tmpl.Config, &expected), tc.description)
493		}
494	}

496	func TestEncodeScriptReason(t *testing.T) {
497		maxLineLength = 8
498		t.Run("regular script", func(t *testing.T) {
499			reason := encodeScriptReason("0123456\n")
500			assert.Equal(t, reason, "")
501		})
502		t.Run("binary script", func(t *testing.T) {
503			reason := encodeScriptReason("abc\a123")
504			assert.Equal(t, reason, "unprintable character '\\a' at offset 3")
505		})
506		t.Run("contains a tab character", func(t *testing.T) {
507			// newline character is included in character count
508			reason := encodeScriptReason("foo\tbar")
509			assert.Equal(t, reason, "unprintable character '\\t' at offset 3")
510		})
511		t.Run("long line", func(t *testing.T) {
512			// newline character is included in character count
513			reason := encodeScriptReason("line 1\nline 2\n01234567\n")
514			assert.Equal(t, reason, "line 3 (offset 14) is longer than 8 characters")
515		})
516	}

// SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright The Lima Authors
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

package limatmpl

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"reflect"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
	"gotest.tools/v3/assert"
	"gotest.tools/v3/assert/cmp"

	"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/limatype"
	"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/limayaml"
)

type embedTestCase struct {
	description string
	template    string
	base        string
	expected    string
}

// Notes:
// * When the template starts with "#" then the comparison will be textual instead of structural.
//   This is required to verify comment handling.
// * If the description starts with "TODO" then the test is expected to fail (until it is fixed).
// * If the description starts with "ERROR" then the test is expected to fail with an error containing the expected string.
// * base is split on "---\n" and stored as base0.yaml, base1.yaml, ... in the same dir as the template.
// * If any base template starts with "#!" then the extension will be .sh instead of .yaml.
// * The template is automatically prefixed with "base: base0.yaml" unless base0 is a script.
// * All line comments will be separated by 2 spaces from the value on output.
// * Merge order of additionalDisks, mounts, and networks depends on the logic in the
//   combineListEntries() functions and will not follow the order of the base template(s).

var embedTestCases = []embedTestCase{
	{
		"Empty template",
		"",
		"vmType: qemu",
		"vmType: qemu",
	},
	{
		"Base doesn't override existing values",
		"vmType: vz",
		"{arch: aarch64, vmType: qemu}",
		"{arch: aarch64, vmType: vz}",
	},
	{
		"Comments are copied over as well",
		`#
# VM Type is QEMU
vmType: qemu # QEMU
`,
		`
# Arch is x86_64
arch: x86_64 # X86
`,
		`
# VM Type is QEMU
vmType: qemu  # QEMU
# Arch is x86_64
arch: x86_64  # X86
`,
	},
	{
		"mountTypesUnsupported are concatenated and duplicates removed",
		"mountTypesUnsupported: [9p,reverse-sshfs]",
		"mountTypesUnsupported: [9p,virtiofs]",
		"mountTypesUnsupported: [9p,reverse-sshfs,virtiofs]",
	},
	{
		"minimumLimaVersion (including comments) is updated when the base version is higher",
		`#
# Works with Lima 0.8.0 and later
minimumLimaVersion: 0.8.0 # needs 0.8.0
`,
		`
# Requires at least 1.0.2
minimumLimaVersion: 1.0.2    # or later
`,
		`
# Requires at least 1.0.2
minimumLimaVersion: 1.0.2  # or later
`,
	},
	{
		"vmOpts.qmu.minimumVersion is updated when the base version is higher",
		"vmOpts: {qemu: {minimumVersion: 8.2.1}}",
		"vmOpts: {qemu: {minimumVersion: 9.1.0}}",
		"vmOpts: {qemu: {minimumVersion: 9.1.0}}",
	},
	{
		"dns list is not appended, but the highest priority one is picked",
		"dns: [1.1.1.1]",
		"dns: [8.8.8.8, 1.2.3.4]",
		"dns: [1.1.1.1]",
	},
	{
		"Update comments on existing maps and lists that don't have comments yet",
		`#
additionalDisks:
- name: disk1 # One
`,
		`
# Mount additional disks
additionalDisks: # comment
# This is disk2
- name: disk2 # Two
`,
		`
# Mount additional disks
additionalDisks:  # comment
- name: disk1  # One
# This is disk2
- name: disk2  # Two
`,
	},
	{
		"probes and provision scripts are prepended instead of appended",
		"probes: [{script: 1}]\nprovision: [{script: One}]",
		"probes: [{script: 2}]\nprovision: [{script: Two}]",
		"probes: [{script: 2},{script: 1}]\nprovision: [{script: Two},{script: One}]",
	},
	{
		"additionalDisks append, but merge fields on shared name",
		"additionalDisks: [{name: disk1}]",
		"additionalDisks: [{name: disk2},{name: disk1, format: true}]",
		"additionalDisks: [{name: disk1, format: true},{name: disk2}]",
	},
	{
		// This test fails because there are 2 spurious newlines in the merged output
		"TODO mounts append, but merge fields on shared mountPoint",
		`#
# My mounts
mounts:
- location: loc1  # mountPoint loc1
- location: loc1
  mountPoint: loc2
`,
		`
mounts:
# will update mountPoint loc2
- location: loc1
  mountPoint: loc2
  writable: true
  # SSHFS
  sshfs:  # ssh
    followSymlinks: true
# will create new mountPoint loc3
- location: loc1
  mountPoint: loc3
  writable: true
`,
		`
# My mounts
mounts:
- location: loc1  # mountPoint loc1
# will update mountPoint loc2
- location: loc1
  mountPoint: loc2
  writable: true
  # SSHFS
  sshfs:  # ssh
    followSymlinks: true
# will create new mountPoint loc3
- location: loc1
  mountPoint: loc3
  writable: true
`,
	},
	{
		// This entry can be deleted when the previous one no longer fails
		"mounts append, but merge fields on shared mountPoint (no comments version)",
		`mounts: [{location: loc1}, {location: loc1, mountPoint: loc2}]`,
		`mounts: [{location: loc1, mountPoint: loc2, writable: true, sshfs: {followSymlinks: true}}, {location: loc1, mountPoint: loc3, writable: true}]`,
		`mounts: [{location: loc1}, {location: loc1, mountPoint: loc2, writable: true, sshfs: {followSymlinks: true}}, {location: loc1, mountPoint: loc3, writable: true}]`,
	},
	{
		"template: URLs are not embedded when embedAll is false",
		// also tests file.url format
		``,
		`
base: template:default
provision:
- file:
    url: template:provision.sh
probes:
- file:
    url: template:probe.sh
`,
		`
base: template:default
provision:
- file: template:provision.sh
probes:
- file: template:probe.sh
`,
	},
	{
		"ERROR Each template must only be embedded once",
		`#
arch: aarch64
`,
		`
base: base0.yaml
# failure would mean this test loops forever, not that it fails the test
vmType: qemu
`,
		`base template loop detected`,
	},
	{
		"ERROR All bases following template: bases must be template: URLs too when embedAll is false",
		``,
		`base: [template:default, base1.yaml]`,
		"after not embedding",
	},
	{
		"ERROR All bases following template: bases must be template: URLs too when embedAll is false",
		``,
		`
base: [base1.yaml, base2.yaml]
---
base: template:default
---
base: baseX.yaml`,
		"after not embedding",
	},
	{
		"Bases are embedded depth-first",
		`#`,
		`
base: [base1.yaml, {url: base2.yaml}] # also test file.url format
additionalDisks: [disk0]
---
base: base3.yaml
additionalDisks: [disk1]
---
additionalDisks: [disk2]
---
additionalDisks: [disk3]
`,
		`
additionalDisks: [disk0, disk1, disk3, disk2]
`,
	},
	{
		"additionalDisks with name '*' are merged with all previous entries",
		`
additionalDisks:
- name: disk1
- name: disk2
- name: disk3
  format: false
`,
		`
additionalDisks:
- name: disk4
- name: "*"
  format: true # will apply to disk1, disk2, and disk4
- name: disk5
`,
		`
additionalDisks:
- name: disk1
  format: true
- name: disk2
  format: true
- name: disk3
  format: false
- name: disk4
  format: true
- name: disk5
`,
	},
	{
		// This test fails because the yq commands don't handle comments properly; may need to be fixed in yq
		"TODO additionalDisks will be upgraded from string to map",
		`#
additionalDisks:
# my head comment
- mine # my line comment
`,
		`
# head comment
additionalDisks: # line comment
- name: "*"
  format: true # formatting is good for you
`,
		`
# head comment
additionalDisks:  # line comment
# my head comment
- name: mine  # my line comment
  format: true  # formatting is good for you
`,
	},
	{
		// This entry can be deleted when the previous one no longer fails
		"additionalDisks will be upgraded from string to map (no comments version)",
		`additionalDisks: [mine]`,
		`additionalDisks: [{name: "*", format: true}]`,
		`additionalDisks: [{name: mine, format: true}]`,
	},
	{
		"networks without interface name are not merged",
		`
networks:
- interface: lima1
`,
		`
networks:
- interface: lima2
# The metric will not be merged with anything
- metric: 250
- interface: lima1
  metric: 100     # will be set on the first entry
- interface: '*'  # wildcard
  metric: 123     # will be set on the first entry
`,
		`
networks:
- interface: lima1
  metric: 100  # will be set on the first entry
- interface: lima2
  metric: 123  # will be set on the first entry
# The metric will not be merged with anything
- metric: 250
`,
	},
	{
		"Scripts are embedded with comments moved",
		`#
# Hi There!
provision:
# This script will be merged from an external file
- file: base1.sh # This comment will move to the "script" key
# This is just a data file
- mode: data
  file: base1.sh # This comment will move to the "content" key
  path: /tmp/data
- mode: yq
  file: base1.sh # This comment will move to the "expression" key
  path: /tmp/yq
`,
		`
# base0.yaml is ignored
---
#!/usr/bin/env bash
echo "This is base1.sh"
`,
		// TODO: the empty line after the `path` is unexpected
		`
# Hi There!
provision:
# This script will be merged from an external file
- script: |-  # This comment will move to the "script" key
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    echo "This is base1.sh"
# This is just a data file
- mode: data
  content: |-  # This comment will move to the "content" key
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    echo "This is base1.sh"
  path: /tmp/data
- mode: yq
  expression: |-  # This comment will move to the "expression" key
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    echo "This is base1.sh"
  path: /tmp/yq

# base0.yaml is ignored
`,
	},
	{
		"Script files are embedded even when no base property exists",
		"provision: [{file: base0.sh}]",
		"#! my script",
		`provision: [{script: "#! my script"}]`,
	},
	{
		"ERROR base digest is not yet implemented",
		"",
		"base: [{url: base.yaml, digest: deafbad}]",
		"not yet implemented",
	},
	{
		"Image URLs will be converted into a template",
		"",
		"base: https://example.com/lima-linux-riscv64.img",
		"{arch: riscv64, os: Linux, images: [{location: https://example.com/lima-linux-riscv64.img, arch: riscv64}]}",
	},
	{
		"Binary files are base64 encoded",
		`#
provision:
- mode: data
  file: base1.sh # This comment will move to the "content" key
  path: /tmp/data
`,
		// base1.sh is binary because it contains an audible bell character '\a'
		"# base0.yaml is ignored\n---\n#!\a123456789012345678901234567890123456789012345678901234567890",
		`
provision:
- mode: data
  content: !!binary |  # This comment will move to the "content" key
    IyEHMTIzNDU2Nzg5MDEyMzQ1Njc4OTAxMjM0NTY3ODkwMTIzNDU2Nzg5MDEyMzQ1Njc4OTAxMjM0
    NTY3ODkw
  path: /tmp/data

# base0.yaml is ignored
`,
	},
}

func TestEmbed(t *testing.T) {
	focus := os.Getenv("TEST_FOCUS")
	for _, tc := range embedTestCases {
		if focus != "" {
			if !strings.Contains(tc.description, focus) {
				continue
			}
			logrus.SetLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)
		}
		t.Run(tc.description, func(t *testing.T) { RunEmbedTest(t, tc) })
	}
	logrus.SetLevel(logrus.InfoLevel)
}

func RunEmbedTest(t *testing.T, tc embedTestCase) {
	todo := strings.HasPrefix(tc.description, "TODO")
	expectError := strings.HasPrefix(tc.description, "ERROR")
	stringCompare := strings.HasPrefix(tc.template, "#")

	// Normalize testcase data
	tc.template = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(tc.template, "#"))
	tc.base = strings.TrimSpace(tc.base)
	tc.expected = strings.TrimSpace(tc.expected)

	// Change to temp directory so all template and script names don't include a slash.
	t.Chdir(t.TempDir())

	for i, base := range strings.Split(tc.base, "---\n") {
		extension := ".yaml"
		if strings.HasPrefix(base, "#!") {
			extension = ".sh"
		}
		baseFilename := fmt.Sprintf("base%d%s", i, extension)
		err := os.WriteFile(baseFilename, []byte(base), 0o600)
		assert.NilError(t, err, tc.description)
	}
	tmpl := &Template{
		Bytes:   fmt.Appendf(nil, "base: base0.yaml\n%s", tc.template),
		Locator: "tmpl.yaml",
	}
	// Don't include `base` property if base0 is a script
	if strings.HasPrefix(tc.base, "#!") {
		tmpl.Bytes = []byte(tc.template)
	}
	err := tmpl.Embed(t.Context(), false, false)
	if expectError {
		assert.ErrorContains(t, err, tc.expected, tc.description)
		return
	}
	assert.NilError(t, err, tc.description)

	if stringCompare {
		actual := strings.TrimSpace(string(tmpl.Bytes))
		if todo {
			assert.Assert(t, actual != tc.expected, tc.description)
		} else {
			assert.Equal(t, actual, tc.expected, tc.description)
		}
		return
	}

	err = tmpl.Unmarshal()
	assert.NilError(t, err, tc.description)

	var expected limatype.LimaYAML
	err = limayaml.Unmarshal([]byte(tc.expected), &expected, "expected")
	assert.NilError(t, err, tc.description)

	if todo {
		// using reflect.DeepEqual because cmp.DeepEqual can't easily be negated
		assert.Assert(t, !reflect.DeepEqual(tmpl.Config, &expected), tc.description)
	} else {
		assert.Assert(t, cmp.DeepEqual(tmpl.Config, &expected), tc.description)
	}
}

func TestEncodeScriptReason(t *testing.T) {
	maxLineLength = 8
	t.Run("regular script", func(t *testing.T) {
		reason := encodeScriptReason("0123456\n")
		assert.Equal(t, reason, "")
	})
	t.Run("binary script", func(t *testing.T) {
		reason := encodeScriptReason("abc\a123")
		assert.Equal(t, reason, "unprintable character '\\a' at offset 3")
	})
	t.Run("contains a tab character", func(t *testing.T) {
		// newline character is included in character count
		reason := encodeScriptReason("foo\tbar")
		assert.Equal(t, reason, "unprintable character '\\t' at offset 3")
	})
	t.Run("long line", func(t *testing.T) {
		// newline character is included in character count
		reason := encodeScriptReason("line 1\nline 2\n01234567\n")
		assert.Equal(t, reason, "line 3 (offset 14) is longer than 8 characters")
	})
}

content.text:
Now I see there's a test case on lines 98-102:

```go
{
    "dns list is not appended, but the highest priority one is picked",
    "dns: [1.1.1.1]",
    "dns: [8.8.8.8, 1.2.3.4]",
    "dns: [1.1.1.1]",
},
```

This test case verifies that when the TEMPLATE has DNS, the base's DNS is NOT appended (it's ignored). But there's no reverse test case: where the TEMPLATE does NOT have DNS but the base template DOES have DNS.

So the existing test only verifies that when the template has DNS, the base DNS is NOT picked up. But the bug is that when the template does NOT have DNS, the base DNS is ALSO not picked up.

Let me now look at the yq `mergeDocuments` script more carefully. The key line:

```
| $a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)
```

This deletes DNS from $b and $c ONLY when $a has .dns. This is to prevent the base DNS from being appended. But then the later lines (line 245-248) also append lists. Let me trace through:

If $a does NOT have .dns:
1. Line 228 does nothing ($a doesn't have .dns)
2. $c has .dns from $b (the base DNS)
3. Line 239: $a * $c copies .dns from $c to $a
4. Line 245: `| $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (path[0] | test("^(base|provision|probes)$") | not))) |= (. + (path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])))` 
   - This finds all "!!seq" tagged nodes in $a (except base, provision, probes)
   - But wait: .dns in $a was copied from $c, and line 232 tagged all lists in $c with "!!tag", then line 261 clears them back. But line 232 only happened on $c, not on $a. After line 239 merges, $a.dns has "!!tag" tag. Then line 261 untags it.
   - Hmm, but the order matters. Line 261 is the LAST line. Line 245 runs BEFORE line 261. So when line 245 runs, $a.dns still has "!!tag" (from the merge). "!!tag" is NOT "!!seq", so line 245 does NOT match $a.dns.

Wait, I need to reorder the lines in `mergeDocuments`:

```
211: const mergeDocuments = `
212:   select(document_index == 0) as $a
213: | select(document_index == 1) as $b
214: 
215: # $c will be mutilated to implement our own "merge only new fields" logic.
216: | $b as $c
217: 
218: # Delete the base that is being merged right now
219: | $a | select(.base | tag == "!!seq") | del(.base[0])
220: | $a | select(.base | (tag == "!!seq" and length == 0)) | del(.base)
221: | $a | select(.base | tag == "!!str") | del(.base)
222: 
223: # If $a.base is a list, then $b.base must be a list as well
224: # (note $b, not $c, because we merge lists from $b)
225: | $b | select((.base | tag == "!!str") and ($a.base | tag == "!!seq")) | .base = [ "" + .base ]
226: 
227: # Delete base DNS entries if the template list is not empty.
228: | $a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)
229: 
230: # Mark all new list fields with a custom tag. This is needed to avoid appending
231: # newly copied lists to themselves again when we merge lists.
232: | $c | .. | select(tag == "!!seq") tag = "!!tag"
233: 
234: # Delete all nodes in $c that are in $a and not a map. This is necessary because
235: # the yq "*n" operator (merge only new fields) does not copy all comments across.
236: | $c | delpaths([$a | .. | select(tag != "!!map") | path])
237: 
238: # Merging with null returns null; use an empty map if $c has only comments
239: | $a * ($c // {}) as $a
240: 
241: # Find all elements that are existing lists. This will not match newly
242: # copied lists because they have a custom !!tag instead of !!seq.
243: # Append the elements from the same path in $b.
244: # Exception: base templates, provision scripts and probes are prepended instead.
245: | $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (path[0] | test("^(base|provision|probes)$") | not))) |=
246:    (. + (path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])))
247: | $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (path[0] | test("^(base|provision|probes)$")))) |=
248:    ((path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])) + .)
249: 
250: # Copy head and line comments for existing lists that do not already have comments.
251: # New lists and existing maps already have their comments updated by the $a * $c merge.
252: | $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (key | head_comment == "")) | key) head_comment |=
253:    (((path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])) | key | head_comment) // "")
254: | $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (key | line_comment == "")) | key) line_comment |=
255:    (((path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])) | key | line_comment) // "")
256: 
257: # Make sure mountTypesUnsupported elements are unique.
258: | $a | (select(.mountTypesUnsupported) | .mountTypesUnsupported) |= unique
259: 
260: # Remove the custom tags again so they do not clutter up the YAML output.
261: | $a | .. | select(tag == "!!tag") tag = ""
262: `
```

OK so the flow is:

1. Line 228: If $a has .dns, delete .dns from $b and $c → This is correct: if the template already sets DNS, don't use base DNS.

2. Line 239: Merge $c into $a (new fields only). Since $a.dns doesn't exist (when template has no DNS), $a gets $c.dns which is a copy of $b.dns.

3. Line 245: `$a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and ...))` - This looks for "!!seq" tagged nodes in $a. But after line 239, $a.dns was copied from $c, which had been tagged as "!!tag" on line 232. So $a.dns has "!!tag", not "!!seq". So LINE 245 DOES NOT MATCH $a.dns. Good, no duplication.

4. Line 261: All "!!tag" in $a are cleared. Now $a.dns has the proper "!!seq" tag again.

So after `mergeDocuments`, $a.dns should have the values from $b.dns (the base DNS). This should work!

But then why doesn't DNS propagate from the base? Let me think about this more...

Wait, let me re-read the bug report carefully:

Main template:
```yaml
base:
  - base/debian.yaml
```

The `base/debian.yaml` is a relative path. Let me check how this is resolved. In `embedAllBases`:

```go
baseLocator := tmpl.Config.Base[0]
```

And then:
```go
base, err := Read(ctx, "", baseLocator.URL)
```

In `Read`, if the locator is not an URL, not a template URL, it's treated as a file path. The base template would be read from the file system. The locator `base/debian.yaml` is relative to the current working directory.

But what about the `FillDefault` function? The `FillDefault` comment says:

```go
//   - DNS are picked from the highest priority where DNS is not empty.
```

And the implementation is:
```go
if len(y.DNS) == 0 {
    y.DNS = d.DNS
}
if len(o.DNS) > 0 {
    y.DNS = o.DNS
}
```

Hmm, I wonder... when the yq merge produces output, and then `limayaml.Unmarshal` parses it, does the DNS field show up correctly?

Let me write a test to verify:</think>Now let me write a test to reproduce the bug. Let me create a test that checks whether DNS from a base template is propagated to the main template:

=== RUN   TestEmbed/Bases_are_embedded_depth-first
=== RUN   TestEmbed/additionalDisks_with_name_'*'_are_merged_with_all_previous_entries
=== RUN   TestEmbed/TODO_additionalDisks_will_be_upgraded_from_string_to_map
=== RUN   TestEmbed/additionalDisks_will_be_upgraded_from_string_to_map_(no_comments_version)
=== RUN   TestEmbed/networks_without_interface_name_are_not_merged

package limatmpl

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"testing"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
	"gotest.tools/v3/assert"
	"gotest.tools/v3/assert/cmp"

	"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/limatype"
	"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/limayaml"
)

func TestDNSSettingPropagation(t *testing.T) {
	logrus.SetLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)

	// Create temp dir
	dir := t.TempDir()
	t.Chdir(dir)

	// Create base template with DNS
	baseYAML := "dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]\n" +
		"hostResolver:\n" +
		"  enabled: false\n" +
		"  ipv6: true\n" +
		"  hosts:\n" +
		"    myhost: myhost.lima.internal\n"

	err := os.WriteFile("base0.yaml", []byte(baseYAML), 0o600)
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to write base template")

	// Main template with only base reference, no DNS
	mainYAML := "base: base0.yaml\n"
	tmpl := &Template{
		Bytes:   []byte(mainYAML),
		Locator: "tmpl.yaml",
	}

	err = tmpl.Embed(t.Context(), false, false)
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to embed")

	err = tmpl.Unmarshal()
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to unmarshal")

	// Check DNS is propagated
	t.Logf("DNS: %v", tmpl.Config.DNS)
	assert.Assert(t, len(tmpl.Config.DNS) == 2, fmt.Sprintf("Expected 2 DNS entries, got %d: %v", len(tmpl.Config.DNS), tmpl.Config.DNS))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[0].String() == "1.1.1.1", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 1.1.1.1, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[0]))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[1].String() == "8.8.8.8", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 8.8.8.8, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[1]))

	// Check hostResolver is propagated
	t.Logf("hostResolver: %+v", tmpl.Config.HostResolver)
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled != nil, "hostResolver.enabled should not be nil")
	assert.Assert(t, *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled == false, fmt.Sprintf("Expected hostResolver.enabled=false, got %v", *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled))
	assert.Assert(t, *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.IPv6 == true, fmt.Sprintf("Expected hostResolver.ipv6=true, got %v", *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.IPv6))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Hosts["myhost"] == "myhost.lima.internal", fmt.Sprintf("Expected hosts[myhost]=myhost.lima.internal, got %s", tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Hosts["myhost"]))

	t.Logf("Result YAML:\n%s", string(tmpl.Bytes))
}
GOEOF

echo "Test file created"

package limatmpl

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"testing"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"

	"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/limatype"
	"github.com/lima-vm/lima/v2/pkg/limayaml"
)

func TestDNSSettingPropagation(t *testing.T) {
	logrus.SetLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)

	// Create temp dir
	dir := t.TempDir()
	t.Chdir(dir)

	// Create base template with DNS
	baseYAML := "dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]\n" +
		"hostResolver:\n" +
		"  enabled: false\n" +
		"  ipv6: true\n" +
		"  hosts:\n" +
		"    myhost: myhost.lima.internal\n"

	err := os.WriteFile("base0.yaml", []byte(baseYAML), 0o600)
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to write base template")

	// Main template with only base reference, no DNS
	mainYAML := "base: base0.yaml\n"
	tmpl := &Template{
		Bytes:   []byte(mainYAML),
		Locator: "tmpl.yaml",
	}

	err = tmpl.Embed(t.Context(), false, false)
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to embed")

	err = tmpl.Unmarshal()
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to unmarshal")

	// Check DNS is propagated
	t.Logf("DNS: %v", tmpl.Config.DNS)
	assert.Assert(t, len(tmpl.Config.DNS) == 2, fmt.Sprintf("Expected 2 DNS entries, got %d: %v", len(tmpl.Config.DNS), tmpl.Config.DNS))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[0].String() == "1.1.1.1", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 1.1.1.1, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[0]))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[1].String() == "8.8.8.8", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 8.8.8.8, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[1]))

	// Check hostResolver is propagated
	t.Logf("hostResolver: %+v", tmpl.Config.HostResolver)
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled != nil, "hostResolver.enabled should not be nil")
	assert.Assert(t, *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled == false, fmt.Sprintf("Expected hostResolver.enabled=false, got %v", *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled))
	assert.Assert(t, *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.IPv6 == true, fmt.Sprintf("Expected hostResolver.ipv6=true, got %v", *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.IPv6))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Hosts["myhost"] == "myhost.lima.internal", fmt.Sprintf("Expected hosts[myhost]=myhost.lima.internal, got %s", tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Hosts["myhost"]))

	t.Logf("Result YAML:\n%s", string(tmpl.Bytes))
}

	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[0].String() == "1.1.1.1", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 1.1.1.1, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[0]))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[1].String() == "8.8.8.8", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 8.8.8.8, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[1]))

	// Check hostResolver is propagated
	t.Logf("hostResolver: %+v", tmpl.Config.HostResolver)
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled != nil, "hostResolver.enabled should not be nil")
	assert.Assert(t, *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled == false, fmt.Sprintf("Expected hostResolver.enabled=false, got %v", *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled))
	assert.Assert(t, *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.IPv6 == true, fmt.Sprintf("Expected hostResolver.ipv6=true, got %v", *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.IPv6))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Hosts["myhost"] == "myhost.lima.internal", fmt.Sprintf("Expected hosts[myhost]=myhost.lima.internal, got %s", tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Hosts["myhost"]))

	t.Logf("Result YAML:\n%s", string(tmpl.Bytes))
}

	baseYAML := "dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]\n" +

The debug log shows the yq expression is being evaluated correctly. Let me inspect the intermediate result more closely. Let me check what the yq merge actually produces.

content.text:
Let me debug the merge logic more carefully. Let me add more debug info to the test and also look at what the existing test for "dns list is not appended" expects. Let me look at that test case more carefully.

The existing test:
- template: `dns: [1.1.1.1]`
- base: `dns: [8.8.8.8, 1.2.3.4]`
- expected: `dns: [1.1.1.1]`

The template has DNS, so the base DNS is ignored. This passes.

My test:
- template: `base: base0.yaml` (empty, no DNS)
- base: `dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]`
- expected: DNS should come from base

But it fails — DNS is empty.

Wait, let me look at the test infrastructure more carefully. In `RunEmbedTest`:

```go
tmpl := &Template{
    Bytes:   fmt.Appendf(nil, "base: base0.yaml\n%s", tc.template),
    Locator: "tmpl.yaml",
}
// Don't include `base` property if base0 is a script
if strings.HasPrefix(tc.base, "#!") {
    tmpl.Bytes = []byte(tc.template)
}
```

So when `tc.template` is not a script, the `base: base0.yaml` is auto-prepended. In the existing test case `"dns list is not appended..."`, the template is `dns: [1.1.1.1]`, but the actual template bytes become `base: base0.yaml\ndns: [1.1.1.1]`.

But wait - the `base0.yaml` content IS the base template. So when the existing test case runs:
- Template: `base: base0.yaml\ndns: [1.1.1.1]` → This has `.dns`
- Base (base0.yaml): `dns: [8.8.8.8, 1.2.3.4]` → This has `.dns`

The yq line 228 `| $a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)` triggers because $a has .dns. So $b.dns and $c.dns are deleted. Then the base DNS is not merged. Expected: template DNS wins. ✓

But in my test case:
- Template: `base: base0.yaml\n` (no .dns field)
- Base (base0.yaml): `dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]`

The yq line 228 does NOT trigger because $a doesn't have .dns. Then $c.dns (from $b.dns) should be merged into $a.

But the result shows DNS is empty! So something else is going wrong. Let me look at the result YAML.

input.new_string:
t.Logf("Result YAML:\n%s", string(tmpl.Bytes))
}

func TestDNSPropagationDebug(t *testing.T) {
	// Minimal reproduction - just run the yq expression directly
	yqExpr := `
  select(document_index == 0) as $a
| select(document_index == 1) as $b
| $b as $c
| $a | select(.base | tag == "!!seq") | del(.base[0])
| $a | select(.base | (tag == "!!seq" and length == 0)) | del(.base)
| $a | select(.base | tag == "!!str") | del(.base)
| $b | select((.base | tag == "!!str") and ($a.base | tag == "!!seq")) | .base = [ "" + .base ]
| $a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)
| $c | .. | select(tag == "!!seq") tag = "!!tag"
| $c | delpaths([$a | .. | select(tag != "!!map") | path])
| $a * ($c // {}) as $a
| $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (path[0] | test("^(base|provision|probes)$") | not))) |=
   (. + (path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])))
| $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (path[0] | test("^(base|provision|probes)$")))) |=
   ((path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])) + .)
| $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (key | head_comment == "")) | key) head_comment |=
   (((path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])) | key | head_comment) // "")
| $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (key | line_comment == "")) | key) line_comment |=
   (((path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])) | key | line_comment) // "")
| $a | (select(.mountTypesUnsupported) | .mountTypesUnsupported) |= unique
| $a | .. | select(tag == "!!tag") tag = ""
| $a
`

	// Template (no DNS)
	tmplYAML := "base: base0.yaml\n"
	// Base (has DNS)
	baseYAML := "dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]\n"

	// Fix the locator
	dir := t.TempDir()
	t.Chdir(dir)
	os.WriteFile("base0.yaml", []byte(baseYAML), 0o600)

	t.Logf("Template: %s", tmplYAML)
	t.Logf("Base: %s", baseYAML)

	documents := fmt.Sprintf("%s\n---\n%s", tmplYAML, baseYAML)
	t.Logf("Documents:\n%s", documents)
}

| $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (key | line_comment == "")) | key) line_comment |=
   (((path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])) | key | line_comment) // "")
| $a | (select(.mountTypesUnsupported) | .mountTypesUnsupported) |= unique
| $a | .. | select(tag == "!!tag") tag = ""
| $a
`

	// Template (no DNS)
	tmplYAML := "base: base0.yaml\n"
	// Base (has DNS)
	baseYAML := "dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]\n"

	// Fix the locator
	dir := t.TempDir()
	t.Chdir(dir)
	os.WriteFile("base0.yaml", []byte(baseYAML), 0o600)

	t.Logf("Template: %s", tmplYAML)
	t.Logf("Base: %s", baseYAML)

	documents := fmt.Sprintf("%s\n---\n%s", tmplYAML, baseYAML)
	t.Logf("Documents:\n%s", documents)
}

package limatmpl

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"testing"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
	"gotest.tools/v3/assert"
)

func TestDNSSettingPropagation(t *testing.T) {
	logrus.SetLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)

	// Create temp dir
	dir := t.TempDir()
	t.Chdir(dir)

	// Create base template with DNS
	baseYAML := "dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]\n" +
		"hostResolver:\n" +
		"  enabled: false\n" +
		"  ipv6: true\n" +
		"  hosts:\n" +
		"    myhost: myhost.lima.internal\n"

	err := os.WriteFile("base0.yaml", []byte(baseYAML), 0o600)
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to write base template")

	// Main template with only base reference, no DNS
	mainYAML := "base: base0.yaml\n"
	tmpl := &Template{
		Bytes:   []byte(mainYAML),
		Locator: "tmpl.yaml",
	}

	err = tmpl.Embed(t.Context(), false, false)
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to embed")

	err = tmpl.Unmarshal()
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to unmarshal")

	// Check DNS is propagated
	t.Logf("DNS: %v", tmpl.Config.DNS)
	assert.Assert(t, len(tmpl.Config.DNS) == 2, fmt.Sprintf("Expected 2 DNS entries, got %d: %v", len(tmpl.Config.DNS), tmpl.Config.DNS))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[0].String() == "1.1.1.1", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 1.1.1.1, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[0]))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[1].String() == "8.8.8.8", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 8.8.8.8, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[1]))

	// Check hostResolver is propagated
	t.Logf("hostResolver: %+v", tmpl.Config.HostResolver)
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled != nil, "hostResolver.enabled should not be nil")
	assert.Assert(t, *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled == false, fmt.Sprintf("Expected hostResolver.enabled=false, got %v", *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled))
	assert.Assert(t, *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.IPv6 == true, fmt.Sprintf("Expected hostResolver.ipv6=true, got %v", *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.IPv6))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Hosts["myhost"] == "myhost.lima.internal", fmt.Sprintf("Expected hosts[myhost]=myhost.lima.internal, got %s", tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Hosts["myhost"]))

	t.Logf("Result YAML:\n%s", string(tmpl.Bytes))
}

Actually, let me take a different approach. Let me look at what the yq merge output actually is by looking at the log output of the test:

The debug log shows the merged YAML is being evaluated. But the `.dns` is being lost. Let me trace through more carefully.

Actually, wait. Let me re-read the debug log from the test:

```
time="...Z" level=debug msg="Embedding base \"/tmp/.../base0.yaml\" in template \"tmpl.yaml\""
time="...Z" level=debug msg="interpreting argument \"/tmp/.../base0.yaml\" as a file path for instance \"base0\""
time="...Z" level=debug msg="Embedding templates into \"/tmp/.../base0.yaml\""
```

The base template is `base0.yaml` which has `dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]`. It has no `base` field, so nothing further to embed. Then `tmpl.merge(base)` is called.

Let me re-read the `merge` function:

```go
func (tmpl *Template) merge(base *Template) error {
    if err := tmpl.mergeBase(base); err != nil {
        return tmpl.ClearOnError(err)
    }
    documents := fmt.Sprintf("%s\n---\n%s", string(tmpl.Bytes), string(base.Bytes))
    return tmpl.evalExprImpl(mergeDocuments, []byte(documents))
}
```

So `documents` is the template bytes and base bytes combined. The `mergeDocuments` yq expression references `$a` (document 0 = template) and `$b` (document 1 = base).

Now, the template bytes before merge are:
```
base: /tmp/.../base0.yaml
```

The base bytes are:
```
dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]
```

After `UseAbsLocators()`, the `base` field is converted to an absolute path. 

Now in `mergeDocuments`:
1. Line 219: `$a | select(.base | tag == "!!seq") | del(.base[0])` - This deletes `base[0]` from $a. After this, $a's `.base` is empty list.
2. Line 220: `$a | select(.base | (tag == "!!seq" and length == 0)) | del(.base)` - Since `.base` is now empty, this deletes the entire `.base` field from $a. Now $a is `{}`.
3. Line 228: `$a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)` - $a doesn't have `.dns` (it's `{}`), so nothing happens.
4. Line 239: `$a * ($c // {}) as $a` - $a is `{}`, $c is a copy of $b (with `dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]`). So $a gets `.dns` from $c. Now $a = `{dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]}`. But wait, the `.dns` in $c has been tagged as "!!tag" on line 232. So we get `{dns: !!tag [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]}`.

Hmm wait, but then:
- Line 245: This matches "!!seq" in $a. Since $a.dns has "!!tag" not "!!seq", it does NOT match. Good.
- Line 261: `| $a | .. | select(tag == "!!tag") tag = ""` - This removes the "!!tag" from $a.dns, making it "!!seq" again.

So after the merge, $a should have `dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]`. But the test says the result is empty!

Hmm, wait. The issue might be with how `UseAbsLocators()` works. Let me re-read that.

OK wait, I think I may be overanalyzing this. Let me look at the actual merge function more carefully. Let me check what `mergeDocuments` is doing step by step.

Actually, I just realized something. Let me look at the `merge` function again:

```go
func (tmpl *Template) merge(base *Template) error {
    if err := tmpl.mergeBase(base); err != nil {
        return tmpl.ClearOnError(err)
    }
    documents := fmt.Sprintf("%s\n---\n%s", string(tmpl.Bytes), string(base.Bytes))
    return tmpl.evalExprImpl(mergeDocuments, []byte(documents))
}
```

`mergeBase` handles the `minimumLimaVersion` and `vmOpts.qemu.minimumVersion` comparison. But then the actual merge happens via `evalExprImpl(mergeDocuments, documents)`.

Now, `mergeDocuments` has:
```
const mergeDocuments = `
  select(document_index == 0) as $a
| select(document_index == 1) as $b
```

So $a = document 0 = template (before merge), $b = document 1 = base.

And `documents` is built as `tmpl.Bytes + "\n---\n" + base.Bytes`.

The template bytes at this point (after `UseAbsLocators` and `embedAllBases` on the base) would be:
```yaml
base: /tmp/.../base0.yaml
```

And the base bytes would be:
```yaml
dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]
```

So `mergeDocuments` script is:
1. Line 219: Delete `base[0]` from $a → `$a` becomes `{base: []}`
2. Line 220: Since `.base` is now empty list, delete `.base` → `$a` becomes `{}`
3. Line 228: `$a | select(.dns)` → false (no `.dns`), so DNS stays in $b and $c
4. Line 232: Tag all lists in $c with "!!tag" → `$c.dns` becomes "!!tag"
5. Line 236: Delete from $c nodes that exist in $a → $a is `{}`, nothing to delete from $c
6. Line 239: `$a * ($c // {})` → $a gets `.dns` from $c (tagged as "!!tag")
7. Line 245: Match "!!seq" lists in $a → $a.dns has "!!tag" not "!!seq", so no match
8. Line 261: Clear all "!!tag" → $a.dns becomes "!!seq"

So after the merge, $a SHOULD have `dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]`. But the test shows empty!

Hmm, wait. Actually my test uses the same test infrastructure as `RunEmbedTest`. But `RunEmbedTest` auto-prepends `base: base0.yaml\n` to the template. My test already has `base: base0.yaml\n` explicitly. Is there any issue with how my test sets things up vs the existing test?

Let me look at the `embedTestCases` again. In the existing test, the template bytes are:
```go
Bytes: fmt.Appendf(nil, "base: base0.yaml\n%s", tc.template)
```

When `tc.template` is `"dns: [1.1.1.1]"`, the bytes become:
```
base: base0.yaml
dns: [1.1.1.1]
```

And `base0.yaml` contains `dns: [8.8.8.8, 1.2.3.4]`.

In my test, the bytes are:
```
base: base0.yaml
```

And `base0.yaml` contains `dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]`.

In both cases, the merge should work. Let me add more debugging. Actually, let me just run the existing test case that passes and my new test case side by side to see the difference.

package limatmpl

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"testing"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
	"gotest.tools/v3/assert"
)

func TestDNSSettingPropagation(t *testing.T) {
	logrus.SetLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)

	dir := t.TempDir()
	t.Chdir(dir)

	// Test 1: Base has DNS, template does NOT have DNS - DNS should propagate
	t.Run("dns propagates from base", func(t *testing.T) {
		baseYAML := "dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]\n" +
			"hostResolver:\n" +
			"  enabled: false\n" +
			"  ipv6: true\n" +
			"  hosts:\n" +
			"    myhost: myhost.lima.internal\n"

		err := os.WriteFile("base0.yaml", []byte(baseYAML), 0o600)
		assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to write base template")

		mainYAML := "base: base0.yaml\n"
		tmpl := &Template{
			Bytes:   []byte(mainYAML),
			Locator: "tmpl.yaml",
		}

		err = tmpl.Embed(t.Context(), false, false)
		assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to embed")

		// Check the raw YAML output
		t.Logf("Raw YAML output:\n%s\n---END---", string(tmpl.Bytes))

		err = tmpl.Unmarshal()
		assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to unmarshal")

		t.Logf("DNS: %v (len=%d)", tmpl.Config.DNS, len(tmpl.Config.DNS))
		assert.Assert(t, len(tmpl.Config.DNS) == 2, fmt.Sprintf("Expected 2 DNS entries, got %d: %v", len(tmpl.Config.DNS), tmpl.Config.DNS))
		if len(tmpl.Config.DNS) >= 2 {
			assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[0].String() == "1.1.1.1", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 1.1.1.1, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[0]))
			assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[1].String() == "8.8.8.8", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 8.8.8.8, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[1]))
		}

		t.Logf("hostResolver: %+v", tmpl.Config.HostResolver)
		assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled != nil, "hostResolver.enabled should not be nil")
	})

	// Test 2: Template HAS DNS, base also has DNS - Template DNS should win
	t.Run("template dns overrides base dns", func(t *testing.T) {
		baseYAML := "dns: [8.8.8.8]\n"
		err := os.WriteFile("base1.yaml", []byte(baseYAML), 0o600)
		assert.NilError(t, err)

		mainYAML := "base: base1.yaml\ndns: [1.1.1.1]\n"
		tmpl := &Template{
			Bytes:   []byte(mainYAML),
			Locator: "tmpl2.yaml",
		}

		err = tmpl.Embed(t.Context(), false, false)
		assert.NilError(t, err)

		t.Logf("Raw YAML output:\n%s\n---END---", string(tmpl.Bytes))

		err = tmpl.Unmarshal()
		assert.NilError(t, err)

		t.Logf("DNS: %v (len=%d)", tmpl.Config.DNS, len(tmpl.Config.DNS))
		assert.Assert(t, len(tmpl.Config.DNS) == 1, fmt.Sprintf("Expected 1 DNS entry, got %d: %v", len(tmpl.Config.DNS), tmpl.Config.DNS))
		if len(tmpl.Config.DNS) >= 1 {
			assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[0].String() == "1.1.1.1", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 1.1.1.1, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[0]))
		}
	})
}

package limatmpl

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"testing"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
	"gotest.tools/v3/assert"
)

func TestDNSSettingPropagation(t *testing.T) {
	logrus.SetLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)

	// Create temp dir
	dir := t.TempDir()
	t.Chdir(dir)

	// Create base template with DNS
	baseYAML := "dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]\n" +
		"hostResolver:\n" +
		"  enabled: false\n" +
		"  ipv6: true\n" +
		"  hosts:\n" +
		"    myhost: myhost.lima.internal\n"

	err := os.WriteFile("base0.yaml", []byte(baseYAML), 0o600)
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to write base template")

	// Main template with only base reference, no DNS
	mainYAML := "base: base0.yaml\n"
	tmpl := &Template{
		Bytes:   []byte(mainYAML),
		Locator: "tmpl.yaml",
	}

	err = tmpl.Embed(t.Context(), false, false)
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to embed")

	err = tmpl.Unmarshal()
	assert.NilError(t, err, "failed to unmarshal")

	// Check DNS is propagated
	t.Logf("DNS: %v", tmpl.Config.DNS)
	assert.Assert(t, len(tmpl.Config.DNS) == 2, fmt.Sprintf("Expected 2 DNS entries, got %d: %v", len(tmpl.Config.DNS), tmpl.Config.DNS))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[0].String() == "1.1.1.1", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 1.1.1.1, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[0]))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.DNS[1].String() == "8.8.8.8", fmt.Sprintf("Expected 8.8.8.8, got %s", tmpl.Config.DNS[1]))

	// Check hostResolver is propagated
	t.Logf("hostResolver: %+v", tmpl.Config.HostResolver)
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled != nil, "hostResolver.enabled should not be nil")
	assert.Assert(t, *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled == false, fmt.Sprintf("Expected hostResolver.enabled=false, got %v", *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Enabled))
	assert.Assert(t, *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.IPv6 == true, fmt.Sprintf("Expected hostResolver.ipv6=true, got %v", *tmpl.Config.HostResolver.IPv6))
	assert.Assert(t, tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Hosts["myhost"] == "myhost.lima.internal", fmt.Sprintf("Expected hosts[myhost]=myhost.lima.internal, got %s", tmpl.Config.HostResolver.Hosts["myhost"]))

	t.Logf("Result YAML:\n%s", string(tmpl.Bytes))
}

func TestDNSPropagationDebug(t *testing.T) {
	// Minimal reproduction - just run the yq expression directly
	yqExpr := `
  select(document_index == 0) as $a
| select(document_index == 1) as $b
| $b as $c
| $a | select(.base | tag == "!!seq") | del(.base[0])
| $a | select(.base | (tag == "!!seq" and length == 0)) | del(.base)
| $a | select(.base | tag == "!!str") | del(.base)
| $b | select((.base | tag == "!!str") and ($a.base | tag == "!!seq")) | .base = [ "" + .base ]
| $a | select(.dns) | del($b.dns, $c.dns)
| $c | .. | select(tag == "!!seq") tag = "!!tag"
| $c | delpaths([$a | .. | select(tag != "!!map") | path])
| $a * ($c // {}) as $a
| $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (path[0] | test("^(base|provision|probes)$") | not))) |=
   (. + (path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])))
| $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (path[0] | test("^(base|provision|probes)$")))) |=
   ((path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])) + .)
| $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (key | head_comment == "")) | key) head_comment |=
   (((path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])) | key | head_comment) // "")
| $a | (.. | select(tag == "!!seq" and (key | line_comment == "")) | key) line_comment |=
   (((path[] as $p ireduce ($b; .[$p])) | key | line_comment) // "")
| $a | (select(.mountTypesUnsupported) | .mountTypesUnsupported) |= unique
| $a | .. | select(tag == "!!tag") tag = ""
| $a
`

	// Template (no DNS)
	tmplYAML := "base: base0.yaml\n"
	// Base (has DNS)
	baseYAML := "dns: [1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8]\n"

	// Fix the locator
	dir := t.TempDir()
	t.Chdir(dir)
	os.WriteFile("base0.yaml", []byte(baseYAML), 0o600)

	t.Logf("Template: %s", tmplYAML)
	t.Logf("Base: %s", baseYAML)

	documents := fmt.Sprintf("%s\n---\n%s", tmplYAML, baseYAML)
	t.Logf("Documents:\n%s", documents)
}
